page: Microsoft Azure A1_v2 is an entry-level virtual machine belonging to the Av2 server family. It is configured with 1 dedicated vCPU based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8171M processor running at 2.6 GHz. This single-core, single-threaded setup is paired with 2.0 GB of system memory and 10 GB of local storage. The underlying virtualization is managed by Microsoft Hyper-V. The CPU includes instruction set extensions such as AVX, AVX-512F, AES, BMI2, ADX, and FMA, though it lacks newer deep learning extensions like AVX-512 VNNI or AMX.

Benchmark data places the performance of the A1_v2 in the bottom 10% across all tested categories. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests both yield a score of 331.25. Memory performance is constrained, with a read-only small block bandwidth of 28.82 GB/sec and uncached large block read and write bandwidths of 3.72 GB/sec and 3.18 GB/sec, respectively. Cryptographic and database operations are also limited, as evidenced by a Passmark CPU encryption score of 108.34 and a database operations score of 180.31. Redis SET operations achieve 0.45 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is measured at 0.45 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the A1_v2 represents a low-resource tier designed for minimal workloads where low cost is prioritized over compute density or high throughput. Due to its limited memory capacity of 2.0 GB and low benchmark scores, it is not suitable for memory-intensive databases, complex data science jobs, or heavy web serving. Instead, the server is best fitted for basic utility tasks, light development or testing environments, simple scripting, and low-traffic static web hosting.
description: Microsoft Azure A1_v2 is an entry-level virtual machine within the Av2 family, featuring 1 dedicated vCPU and 2.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8171M processor running at 2.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 10 GB of local storage and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Performance benchmarks place this instance in the bottom 10% across all metrics, with a stress-ng CPU score of 331.25 and a Passmark database operations score of 180.31. Memory bandwidth is limited, reaching 28.82 GB/sec for small cached blocks and dropping to 3.72 GB/sec for larger uncached blocks. Given these hardware constraints, the A1_v2 is positioned as a highly economical option for low-demand workloads. It is best suited for basic administrative tasks, light development or testing, simple scripting, and low-traffic static web serving rather than resource-intensive applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure A1_v2 is a budget-tier virtual machine featuring 1 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPU, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 10 GB of local storage, designed for low-demand utility workloads and basic development tasks.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure A1_v2 is a low-resource virtual machine with 1 Intel Xeon vCPU, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 10 GB of local storage for basic utility tasks.
tagline: An entry-level, single-core virtual machine with minimal memory and local storage designed for basic utility tasks and light development.
bullet_points: Single-core Intel Xeon 8171M processor running at 2.6 GHz
2.0 GB of system memory and 10 GB of local storage
Dedicated CPU allocation on Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor
Low-tier benchmark performance across compute and memory tests
Best suited for light development, testing, and basic scripting
